data.content: The problem is clear. The `GenerateSampleCode` target generates the file but never adds it to the `Compile` item group, so Roslyn doesn't know about it. The fix is to include the generated file in compilation:

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add the generated file to compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. The `GenerateSampleCode` target runs `BeforeTargets="CoreCompile;BeforeCompile"` and generates the file, but it never adds the generated file to the `Compile` item group. The MSBuild compilation has already gathered its source files by the time `CoreCompile` runs, so the generated file isn't included.

The fix is to add the generated file to the `Compile` item group within the target, using `<ItemGroup>` inside the target:

```xml
<ItemGroup>
  <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
</ItemGroup>
```


This needs to be placed right after the file is written, inside the `GenerateSampleCode` target.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add the generated file to compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

  </Target>

data.content:
Build succeeds now. 

**Diagnosis:** The `GenerateSampleCode` target wrote the `.cs` file to disk but never added it to the `Compile` item group. By the time `CoreCompile` runs, MSBuild has already resolved which source files to pass to Roslyn — so the generated file was simply ignored.

**Fix:** Added `<Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />` inside the target so the generated file is included in compilation.
